VerbalDescription: Copy from input to match the grid and original shapes. Choose a matching color and fill in a square in the middle of each shape.
Change grid size as in the input grid. Then fill out the grid with the same color as shown in the input grid.
INCLUDE THE NEW CELL I THE CENTER
Copy the input grid to the output grid (including resizing to match); place a cell of the same color in the center of each square outline.
Copy grid. Add a matching square to the center to the feature.
Copy the image exactly for the output.  Then, directly in the middle of the colored square outline, put one block of that same color directly in the middle.

keep the grid size the same
locate all the squares in the original grid. 
copy each square to the new grid - color, size, and location should all be the same. 
color exactly one space in the center of each square.  the space should be the same color as the square itself
Resize the grid to the same as the input, then if the shape is hollow put a dot the same colour in the middle. If the dot is shaded the put a black dot on the middle
blank tha gird fill,fill tha grid blank
point the centre of the box in respctive box colour
Add a solid square that is the same color of the figures to each figure.  Place the square directly in the middle of the new figure.
First, completely copy the original. Then, add a square of the same color within the squares created.
Copy test input grid and change the colour cell at the centre of each of the squares to the same colour as the squares.
Put a single square of the matching colour in the very centre of each square present, even if that makes it a solid shape.
Add a same colored square into the center of each shape
The objects and grid remain the same. The change is that a single square is added to each in the direct middle and it is the same color of each object/it matches.
Copy the same objects then add one grid square in the middle of each object with their corresponding color.
Keep the grid size the same. Keep the colors the same. Just add a square in the middle of all 4 objects. Keep the same color as the objects.

Resize your grid to the size of the input grid. Duplicate all the objects from that grid into your area. At the very center of each object, color one space with the color of the object surrounding that space.
Put a single blue square exactly diagonally in the center between the shapes.

Copy the original input so that you start with the exact same grid dimensions, shapes, and colors.  Then you will simply need to place a single blue square exactly in between the two original shapes so that it is an equal length and width from each shape.
i follow the Task demonstration
Directly in the center of each pair of shapes add a blue pixel.
grid and color stay the same. 2 opposing shapes are placed in the same spot on the grid. imagine a ball passed between them. place a blue block between them at the center point
Copy the grid to your answer grid. you need to select the dark blue colour and colour in the square that is the centre of the two shapes. then submit.

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You be shown two grey shapes and what you need to do is to place a blue unit square in the middle of the shapes (so that it is an equal distance from both shapes.)
On the board there are two gray shapes. The rule is to place a blue square in the center of the gray shapes, as they will always be spaced evenly
copy the grid exactly and then make the cetral sqaure blue
I fill the color based on instruction.
Place a blue cube in the middle point between the two shapes.
Place one blue square symmetrically spaced between both objects
Copy test input grid, highlight a cell exactly half way between the grey shapes and change its colour to blue.
Using the same size grid as the input, recreate the grey shapes exactly and then place a single blue block in the exaxt diagonal center between the two grey shapes
Resize it the same way. Color it the same way. Color blue right in the middle of the grey patterns.
Put one blue square in the direct middle (both horizontally and vertically) of the two grey shapes in the test input.

Copy over the image and keep objects the same color and in the same spot. You want to find the middle of the two objects and mark it with a blue square. Think of it as a pong travelling back and forth

Keep the same grid size as the input. Your answer should keep the two grey objects in the same positions and colours as the input. Your answer should also include a 1x1 blue square placed directly inbetween the two grey objects

Use the same grid size as the original. There are two objects in the original, they are both grey in colour. Copy the exact size, colour and position of both grey blocks into your answer. Then, add a 1x1 blue object in the forth row down, third column from the left.
The blue square goes between the gray shapes.
Change the grid size to match the original. Copy the image but add a blue square into the middle of the two objects.
Keep the grid the same as the test grid. Place a single blue square at the point where rotational symmetry would be.
first copy the task to your solution. fill in grid 1x1 and using same color in demonstration at the place you consider interception of both shapes.
Find the color in the middle and use just that in a 1x1 grid.
1x1 grid with the same color from the middle of the line
there is a line of colours and the final should be the center colour, the grid is just one cell and the colour of the center cell from the input
take the colour that is in the middle of the sequence and this is the colour you use, and this will only be size 1x1
Change the size of the grid to 1x1. Then fill it in the same colour as the square in the center of the line.
Select the colour that is in the middle of the input. Only output that colour as a 1x1 grid.
I follow the task demonstration
In the line of blocks, find the center most block. Change your gird size to 1x1. Color that block the color of the center most block.
Make a 1 by 1 grid and color it the same color as the middle square of the input object.
Select correct number of squares. Fill in square. Choose the color of middle block.

resize the grid to 1 x 1
the original shape should be a horizontal line with an odd number of spaces.  find the space that is exactly in the middle of the line.  your 1 x 1 square should be the same color as the middle space.
Find the centre colour. Resize grid to 1x1 and fill with this colour.
Take the middle box and make a 1x1 grid with that colour.
Find the colour in the middle and just put that shape on only. So grid to be resized to 1x1, then filled with the colour in the middle
Only the middle square of the input should remain.
You want to resize the grid so that it is only one box or 1X1 and make that box the same color as the one in the center.
Identify the middle square of the input. Creat a one square grid and color it to match the middle square of the input object.
Grid height and width should both be one (1). Grid colour should be the colour of the grid at the middle in the test input
Identify the middle grid square in your input which resembles one row of squares (colors vary). Only submit this middle grid square, nothing else, as your solution. Keep its original color.
You've got a horizontal line. The solution is a single 1x1 square the colour of the middle square of the line.
Change the grid size to 1x1 and find the middle square colour. Set as this colour.
Use the inside shape to make your new grid. The size of the grid will be the same as the shape inside the larger shape.
The input will have several objects. Identify the most central object in the grid. Then, the output grid size will be the size of this object. In this grid, identically replicate this object.
Focus on the single colour shape closest to the centre of the grid.  Change the grid size to the size of this shape and copy it exactly.
Find the complete object in the middle of the input grid. Resize the grid so that it has the dimensions of that object, and copy it into the grid. Ensure to keep the same colours
Look for the most central image. Make the grid that size, remove all other shapes/colours from around it. Keep only the central image.

The coloured shape that makes up the centre of the larger shape must be transferred into the new grid, which will take the dimensions of this shape. Counting both across and down the larger shape to get to the exact middle of the larger square, this will be the shape that you need to copy over. Any interlocking shapes within this shape, e,g a green rectangle that interlocks with a yellow rectangle that is exactly in the middle must not be copied over.
Resized the grid and copied the pattern from the centre of the demonstration grid
Using only the shape in the middle, make the grid this size and fill with the colour that is already the majority of this
Resize the grid to the shape inside the very center of the puzzle, and make that shape the entire grid.

Look at the shapes presented in the input grid. There should be one shape that is touching two other shapes. Resize the output grid to the dimensions of that shape, and then replicate that shape exactly.
find the centermost shape inside of the large square, the final grid should fit the size of this shape and remain the same colour

There should be an array of shapes inside a bigger shape on your input. At the centre of the bigger shape there should be a shape. Make your submission grid be the same size as the shape and copy the shape into your submission grid. Make sure to keep it the same colour

Find the shape which is exactly in the centre of the colour perimeter. Change the grid size to what matches the shape in the centre and then recreate the shape exactly as the middle shape show in the input grid.
Find object in the middle of the grid. Resize grid to the size of that object. Copy that object and any squares inside of that object into the grid.
Make your grid the same dimensions as the dimensions of the center object from the input grid.  Replicate the center object using the same size and color.
There will be multiple shapes scattered around the grid.  Identify the shape directly in the middle.  Copy only that middle shape for the output.  Keep the color the same.
There may be many objects in your initial grid. Create a new grid the same width and height as the central object in your grid. The resulting grid should just hold a recreation of that center object.
Resize the grid to whatever the shape in the center is, and then fill the entire grid with the new center shape.
Pick design in center of example. Resize grid to match. copy center design using same color
Here whatever object is in the centre becomes the focal object. Size your grid to correspond to the width and height of this object. Fill this grid with that central shape in the same color
adjust height and width as the size of center shape, copy center shape it with same shape and colour. if there are any overlapping with other shape, ignore the other shape
sdfgbaeh
Simply remove the object closest to the center, keep grid size and colors the same.

Match the grid size to the size of the original one. Find the object that resides in the middle or find the object that is closest to the middle compared to the other objects. Remove that whole object or block from the grid in fill it in with black blocks. Keep all of the other blocks exactly the same.
Copy the grid and objects exactly.  Change the center cell or cells to black.
copy the input grid size. remove the one shape that is in the middle. keep the rest the same
Press the copy from input button. Remove the shape which is directly in the centre of the grid by changing the colour to black, leave everything else unchanged.

The goal is to identify the block or shape in the center of the grid and remove it, keeping everything else the same. Change the dimensions to 7x7, recreate the green square, but do not add the yellow square in the middle

Make the grid the same height and width as the original. Copy the design using the same color into the same locations on the grid, but leave out the shape in the center of the design. That block or blocks will be black.
Keep everything the same except get rid of the shape located in the center.
Blocks in the middle of the grid are removed.
Delete the shape in the middle. Keep all other shapes the same.

Copy the puzzle.

Remove the block in the center of the grid and any blocks touching this center of the same color 

(Eg if a 3x3 grid where all blocks are red except the center is blue, remove the blue block. If the grid is 9x9 with squares jotted about but a purple block in the center with two blocks above and below the center also in purple, remove this entire shape)
Copy the input grid design and size. Find the coloured block (no matter shape or size) that is central to the design, or closest to centre. Recolour all those squares black.
Copy the grid. Remove any shape that is touching the centre square.
Resize the grid to match the test input grid. Redraw the test input grid apart from the shape which is in the exact middle of the test input grid.

There will be many shapes on the screen, which could be 5x randomly shaded cells, or 3 large shapes. One of these will be the most central, which is where the solutions lies. The solution is to copy the same grid, but then remove (ie shade black) whatever the most central of the coloured shapes/cells is.
use the same grid size as the imput then remove the shape that's in the middle (keep all colors the same)
Copy grid dimensions.  Turn whatever segment of colors that is closest to the middle of the grid black (omit the middle colors).
Find the center most colored shape in the image, and delete it, while keeping the grid size the same
Keeping the same color and size, remove the shape in the center of the rest.
copy all of the shapes as they are apart from the shape which is directly in the middle, everything else is the same, placement and colour.
Keep the same sized grid. Copy the task image exactly, but omit the "middle-most" task shape.

The rule is to remove the center-most shape, no matter the size or color. In this case, you will copy the original grid and 'erase' the center yellow square by choosing 'edit', the black color, and then clicking the yellow square. Leave the rest of the grid how it is.
use exact same grid size, and replicate all the shapes in the same spots EXCEPT for the middle shape, do not put that in.

hit the copy input button
we want to center the two tiles bisected by the large line
black out the two tiles
now center them along the large line keeping the same color
Move the smaller colored dots so that they're surrounding the center most dot of each line.

Resize the grid to match the input box dimensions. There are two colors to the item in the input box. One color is on a line. Reproduce that line in the same color, size, and location as in the input box. The other color is one two individual squares on either side of the other color line. Move those two squares until they are located on either side of the center square of the other color line.
There are two lines in the input box. A long one, and a short one, underneath it. Slide the short one along the long one until it's at the center of the long one.
Use the same grid size. Slide the shorter line along the longer line two blocks, to the right if it is horizontal, and upwards if it's vertical. Keep the same colors.
Use the same grid size as in input. There will be a uneven cross object. Simply move the horizontal squares that form the cross to the middle, making a perfect "plus" sign.
Move the smaller part of the shape to the center.

Keep the grid size the same as the original grid. Keep the larger line of blocks exactly the same. You only have to move the two other colored blocks. If the two smaller blocks are in the center, move them next to the top of the larger object so that it forms a "T" shape. If the two smaller blocks are in any other location to start, move them next to the middle of the larger object so that it forms a cross shape or a lowercase t.

Copy the shape from the test input grid. Black out each square protruding on either side of the colored strip of squares. Using the same color as the cubes you blacked out, color in the square to the left of the central square in the colored strip of squares. Do the same thing for the square to the right of the central square in the strip of squares.
Just copy the grid but move the two dots to the center of the shape. I used the select and fill functions to do this.

There will be a t shape made of two colours. The longer line will be one colour, and there will be one cell either side in a different colour. Keep the grid size the same as the input size and copy the long line in the same shape and colour and position. Add the two cells either side of the line in the same colour, but put them so they are in the middle of the line, as it will originally be off centre.
Copy test input grid. Shift both of the green cells up by one cell.
move the background shape to the middle of the colored line, keeping it in the background
Move the shape in the background to the middle section of the image in the foreground.
Move the shape that is "behind" the shape that is in front of it so that it perfectly intersects with it down the middle of that front most shape.  Keep the grid size and colors the same.

resize the new grid to same dimensions as old grid. 
the design on the old grid should consist of a solid color line with different colored shapes on each side.  
the solid colored line should be copied exactly.  the different colored shapes should be moved 2 spaces right (if line is horizontal) or 2 spaces down (if line is vertical)
Use the same size grid as in the input. Move the smaller object so that it "centers" on to the longest one. Move it (and only the small one) as if you are sliding it to center
10x10 grid, horizontal line spanning 10 squares in yellow, then a blue 2x2 square above and below the line in squares 5 and 6 across.
move the lone colored block to the middle of the shape.
The grid should be copied from the input and stay the same. If there is a lone colored block, move that block to the exact center of the large shape.

Use the same sized grid as the input. Replicate the largest shape exactly in the same place and in the same colour. Move the single square shape to the very middle of the larger shape, keeping it the same colour as it was. There will be two objects.
keep the main shape the same but move the one square to the middle of the larger shape and keep this exactly the same.
Copy from input. Move the square of one colour and put it in the middle of the larger shape. The original position of the one square should now have changed colour to fit in.
resize the grid to 13x13, draw a hollow rectangle in light blue with sizes 5 in height and 9 in width, with the top left corner starting 2 square in from the side and 3 from the top.

Use the Copy from input.
replace the center square of the grid with the same colour as the single square. Replace the single square that is not the in the center with the back ground colour
move the single color block in the middle of the other pattern.

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You will see a shape and one square which has a different colour to the main shape. You will need to move this square so that it is in the centre of the other shape.
copy everything of puzzle (grid height and width, color, shape etc), move the single spot to the center of the coloured shape.
TO INPUT THE CELL DISLOCATED AND ADDIG THE CELL I TO CENTER

Use the same grid size as in the input.  There will be two objects in the input in your answer.  Copy from input and shift the single square object four grid squares to the left and five grid squares down.  Keep the colors the same.
outlying square moves to the centre of the larger shape - mit retains its original colour
Make the grid the same size as the original. Keep the original pattern/shape and keep it's colour. Move the coloured cell to the middle of the pattern, leaving the space where it came from black.
Keep the same grid size. Copy the image from the input. Move the stand alone grid to exact midway of the big shape. Keep the colour the same as in the input.
place the block that is a different colour in the middle of the larger shape, so place the pink block in the middle of the orange shape. nothing else needs to change
Move the square to the center of the other shape.

The grid size stays the same. Copy the first image. There should be a colored piece (or pieces that looks out of place outside of another shape. Move that shape into the exact center of the bigger/main shape, and black out where it was originally.
Copy the input grid and the larger object exactly. then take the smaller input object and move it to the exact middle of the larger object. coloring over the larger object sqares if necessary.

Use the same grid size as in the input (14 by 14). Keep the bigger object at the same position. Then position the small object at the midway grid of the bigger object. Keep the colour the same for each object.
Take the colour from the outside and put it in the middle of the bigger block of colours
Copy the grid size exactly as well as the long blue rectangle, move the yellow square so it is exactly in the middle of the blue rectangle

Make a 14x14 grid, draw the blue shape exactly the same and then put the yellow square in the middle of the blue one(remove the yellow completely from where it is) so you want 4 blue blocks either side of the square to make sure it's in the middle

Find the middle row. For instance, if the input is 5 blocks tall, the middle row is the third row down. Set the output to the dimensions of the colored portion of that row (it should be only 1 tall). Then fill the output with the colors in that middle row. In other words, it's like we're cutting out the colored portion of that middle row, and that's our output.
Find the centre row of the image.  Resize your grid to 1 row high by the number of coloured squares in the centre row long.  Keep the colour the same.

You will see a stairway pattern, possibly upside down. Count how many rows there are in the pattern and find the middle row. Set the height of your grid to one, and the width to the same number of coloured squares that there are in the middle row of your stairway. Colour in every square of your resized grid to the same colour as the stairway.
Resize grid to the height of 1 and width of 2. Take the flood tool and select pink to fill the squares.
Find the row that contains the middle amount of squares. set grid dimensions to a height of 1 and width of how many squares that row contains. Color the grid the same color as the row you selected.
Eliminate the extra dots outside of the zigzag.
Find the average row length. Resize the grid to a height of 1 and the length of the average row. Fill in each square with the same color as the test grid.

There will be several rows of a colour against a black background. Ony recreate the coloured row halfway down the grid. Eg if on a five by five grid, I would only recreate the coloured row in the thrid row of the grid, with none of the black background, only the coloured section.
I looked at the middle of the grid for the input and took how many colourd blocks there were and i made my grid 1 height by that many blocks and filled it all in with that colour.
resize the grid to 1 by 3 and fill in all the squares grey

Identify the center line of the grid. Resize your grid to a height of 1 and the width of the colored line in the test grid. Fill the grid with that original color. You are duplicating that one line without any extra space.
You have a bunch of coloured lines, find the middle line in the grid, make your grid the same size as that coloured line and then colour it in the same colour as the line.

For the solution, find the height of the sample. Divide this number by two to find the middle line. The middle line is the entirety of the solution--keep the color the same and just replicate that middle line in the solution area so that the whole grid is made up of the one line.

Find the height of the grid and then count how many colored spaces are in the middle row.  Resize the new grid to be 1 row high by the number of colored spaces in the middle row wide.  Color the entire new grid the same color as the colored spaces in the demonstration grid.
Duplicate the middle row of gray. No black, just 5 horizontal blocks of gray.
You need to take the middle row of the original shape and create a grid that is just that row (e.g, five rows in the original, you transpose the 3rd row.)

Identify the middle row of the grid and count the number of coloured cells in that row.  Now make a grid that is 1 square high and with a width that matches the number of coloured cells.  Then colour in the whole grid with the same colour as the orginal cells.
Leave the grid the same size and then move the off colored block to the center of the line. Then place a block of the same color above and below that block.
Copy the grid and existing shape. Each shape has a little color "nub" attached to the end of it. Remove the color nub, but draw a (3) grid piece of that color down the middle of the bar line.
For each figure, move the odd block to the center and make a 3 block high cross and keep size and color.

There will be a number of coloured lines with a differently coloured cell at one end. Recreate the lines in the same grid size, except there should now be a three cell high vertical line through the middle of it of the colour of the cell at one end. The cell at one end can be deleted and added to the background.

Use same grid size. Identify the single colour block. Move it to the right so it is above the middle of the long shape. Draw a line (of the single colour) so it goes through the long shape to the other side. Should be a vertical line of three that goes one above the long shape and one below.
follow the instruction

Keep the grid the same size as the input. Replicate any vertical lines in the same colours as the input. Move the coloured square above each vertical line to the middle square and make a vertical line of three squares of this colour that crosses through the horizontal line right through its middle.
Place the single coloured block in the centre of the line then add another square above and below this, in the same colour
All demonstrations showed the only changes were that the single coloured cell moved to the centre with another coloured cell on top and bottom.
Same grid, single block removed and a 3 block line is drawn in the single blocks colour through the centre of any lines

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You will be shown a shape which is in the form of a horizontal line, made up of one colour and one random square which is a completely different colour. You will need to copy this shape as it is, but replace the centre block of the line with the unique square, with its colour unchanged. Also, you should replicate this square twice, once directly above it and once directly below it.

Find the middle of the long line, move the one block square to the middle of the line. Extend it one block on either side. Fill the block in the same color as the original one square box that was moved.

Keep the same grid size. Recreate the the longest color section of the demonstration. At the mid-point of the long colored section take the minority color and make it cross with three blocks of the minority color.

There are lines in the input with a single square of a different color at one end. The output should be a copy of the input of the same size, with the lines in the same place and color. The single squares should be moved to the middle of their respective lines, and extended outward one square. The result is the original lines crossed over at their midpoint by a 3-block line in the other color.

So the rule here seems to be that the opposing coloured block moves to the middle of the dominant colour shape and creates a 3 block cross. So in this case, the orange block at the top of the blue became a 3 block orange cross half way through the blue line of blocks. 

To create this, I first resized the grid to the original image. I then used the edit tool to recreate the blue line. I then changed the colour to the orange, and created a 3 block line across the middle of the blue line.

Resize the grid to the same size as the task input grid. Select the edit button and the appropriate colour. The rule is that the dominant colour must be disected through the exact middle of the shape with the minority colour.

Use the same grid size. 
There will be coloured lines with a random singular colour on one end. Find the centre of the line and using the singular random colour, slice through the line with 3 of the random-coloured squares. This should create a cross-effect. 
Remove the random square from the end of the line.

Use the same size grid as in the input. Keep the "longest" bar in place. Remove the colored dot at the end. Make a 3x1 bar crossing the center of the long bar and make it the same color as the one that was removed.
resize the grid to 11x11, draw a vertical blue line to span 7 squares, starting at 3 down, 5 across, then draw a 1x3 horizontal orange line through the centre.
Copy the input grid. Move the stray pixel of color to the center of the gap between the two lines of color.
Keep the same size grid. Copy the rectangles exactly (without changing colour). Move the square one position towards the furthermost rectangle. Don't change its colour.
Copy the original grid. Now, centralise the dot, in the same color, on the same line, between the two lines.
Move the dot between the two lines to the closest wall.
Copy the grid size of the demonstration and the two long vertical lines, move the single square one tile to the left
make a one grid in the center
the single block between the lines should stay in the same row, and move either up or to the side so its exactly between the lines

There are two parallel lines in the input box, and one colored box somewhere between them. Recreate the grid exactly. Then move the colored box between them. If the lines are vertical, move the box to the side until it's in the row halfway between each line. If the lines are horizontal, move the box up or down until it's midway between the two lines.

Copy from input and select the colour that matches the single coloured square on the grid. If the grid is 6x6 then the single square will move 1 grid place towards the opposite row or coloumn of coloured squares to the one it is closest to. If the grid size is 9x9 then the single square will move 2 grid spaces towards the opposite row or column of coloured squares to the one it is closest to.

Keep the grid size the same. Move the single coloured square into the middle of the shortest space between the two lines. Leave the square in the same position on the longest space between the two lines.

Make the output grid the same size as the input and reproduce the two parallel lines that run from side to side. Move the single square of color between the lines so that it  remains is centered between them, without changing where it lies in the other direction.

The input grid matches the size of the answer grid. Keep the two colored columns or rows the same as the input. The other lone square of a different color must move to be exactly between the two columns or rows.
Copy the boundary lines. Place the single box item to the center of the space between the lines.
Duplicate the grid and then move the red block up one space.

Copy the size of the grid from the input. The single coloured box needs to have the same gap be from the full rows on either side. Move the red box up from so there is a gap of one box. Keep all colours the same
Move the coloured square in between the two lines (or walls) to a position in the middle of the lines (or walls) within the same grid column.
